Please install it via pip.")\n\nclass ScreenshotUtil:\n    """\n    Utility class for capturing screenshots on macOS with Retina scaling support.\n    """\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def capture_screen(region: Optional[Tuple[int, int, int, int]] = None) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specified region.\n        Args:\n            region: (left, top, width, height) or None for full screen.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object of the screenshot.\n        """\n        # Get main display ID\n        display_id = Quartz.CGMainDisplayID()\n        # Get display bounds\n        display_bounds = Quartz.CGDisplayBounds(display_id)\n        width = int(display_bounds.size.width)\n        height = int(display_bounds.size.height)\n\n        # If region is specified, use it; otherwise, capture full screen\n        if region:\n            left, top, w, h = region\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(left, top, w, h)\n        else:\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(0, 0, width, height)\n\n        # Capture the image\n        image_ref = Quartz.CGWindowListCreateImage(\n            capture_rect,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly,\n            Quartz.kCGNullWindowID,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowImageDefault\n        )\n\n        if not image_ref:\n            raise RuntimeError("Failed to capture screen image.")\n\n        # Convert CGImageRef to numpy array\n        width = Quartz.CGImageGetWidth(image_ref)\n        height = Quartz.CGImageGetHeight(image_ref)\n        bytes_per_row = Quartz.CGImageGetBytesPerRow(image_ref)\n        data_provider = Quartz.CGImageGetDataProvider(image_ref)\n        data = Quartz.CGDataProviderCopyData(data_provider)\n        buffer = bytes(data)\n        # RGBA format\n        img_array = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.uint8)\n        img_array = img_array.reshape((height, bytes_per_row // 4, 4))\n        # Remove padding if any\n        img_array = img_array[:, :width, :]\n\n        # Convert to PIL Image\n        pil_img = Image.fromarray(img_array, \'RGBA\')\n        return pil_img\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_retina_scaling(img: Image.Image) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Handles Retina scaling for screenshots.\n        Args:\n            img: PIL.Image object.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object, scaled to standard DPI if needed.\n        """\n        # On Retina displays, images may be 2x or 3x the logical screen size.\n        # We check the DPI and scale down if necessary.\n        # PIL does not always set DPI, so we use image size heuristics.\n\n        # For most macOS Retina screens, scale factor is 2.\n        # If the image mode is RGBA and the size is larger than expected, scale down.\n        # This function assumes the user will pass the full screen image.\n\n        # No direct DPI info, so we just return the image as-is.\n        # If the user wants to scale, they can do so by passing a region or resizing.\n        # For demonstration, let\'s provide a scale-down to 0.5x if the image is likely Retina.\n\n        # Heuristic: If width > 3000, assume Retina and scale down.\n        if img.width > 3000:\n            scaled_img = img.resize((img.width // 2, img.height // 2), Image.LANCZOS)\n            return scaled_img\n        return img\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/quiz_automation_mac_1766905667/hotkey_listener.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport threading\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\nfrom pynput import keyboard\n\n\nclass HotkeyListener:\n    """\n    Listens for a customizable hotkey and triggers the provided callback.\n    Uses pynput for cross-platform hotkey listening (macOS compatible).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, hotkey: str, callback: Callable):\n        """\n        Initializes the hotkey listener.\n        Args:\n            hotkey: Hotkey string, e.g., \'cmd+shift+o\'\n            callback: Function to call when hotkey is pressed\n        """\n        self.hotkey = hotkey.lower()\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.listener: Optional[keyboard.Listener] = None\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._hotkey_set = self._parse_hotkey(self.hotkey)\n        self._pressed_keys = set()\n\n    def _parse_hotkey(self, hotkey_str: str):\n        """\n        Parses the hotkey string into a set of pynput key objects.\n        Supports \'cmd\', \'ctrl\', \'alt\', \'shift\', and single character keys.\n        """\n        key_map = {\n            \'cmd\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'command\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'ctrl\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'control\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'alt\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'option\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'shift\': keyboard.Key.shift,\n        }\n        keys = set()\n        for part in hotkey_str.split(\'+\'):\n            part = part.strip()\n            if part in key_map:\n                keys.add(key_map[part])\n            elif len(part) == 1:\n                keys.add(keyboard.KeyCode.from_char(part))\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported hotkey part: {part}")\n        return keys\n\n    def _on_press(self, key):\n        self._pressed_keys.add(key)\n        if self._hotkey_set.issubset(self._pressed_keys):\n            # Hotkey pressed\n            self.callback()\n\n    def _on_release(self, key):\n        if key in self._pressed_keys:\n            self._pressed_keys.remove(key)\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Starts listening for the hotkey in a non-blocking thread.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self.listener = keyboard.Listener(\n            on_press=self._on_press,\n            on_release=self._on_release\n        )\n        self.listener.start()\n        print(f"[INFO] Hotkey listener started. Waiting for hotkey: {self.hotkey}")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Stops listening for the hotkey.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self.listener is not None:\n            self.listener.stop()\n            self.listener = None\n        print("[INFO] Hotkey listener stopped.")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Example usage: listen for Cmd+Shift+O and print a message\n    def test_callback():\n        print("[HOTKEY] Hotkey pressed! Triggering automation...")\n\n    hotkey = "cmd+shift+o"\n    listener = HotkeyListener(hotkey, test_callback)\n    listener.start_listening()\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            pass  # Keep main thread alive\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        listener.stop_listening()\n        print("Exiting hotkey listener.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Using default config.")\n            return Config.DEFAULT_CONFIG.copy()\n        try:\n            with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                config = json.load(f)\n            # Ensure all default keys are present\n            for key, value in Config.DEFAULT_CONFIG.items():\n                if key not in config:\n                    config[key] = value\n            print(f"[OK] Loaded config from {path}.")\n            return config\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[ERROR] Failed to load config from {path}: {e}. Installing Homebrew...")\n        try:\n            # Official Homebrew install command\n            subprocess.run(\n                \'/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"\',\n                shell=True, check=True)\n            print("[OK] Homebrew installed successfully.")\n            return True\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n            print("[ERROR] Failed to install Homebrew. Please install it manually from https://brew.sh/")\n            return False\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def install_tesseract() -> bool:\n        """\n        Installs Tesseract OCR using Homebrew if not already installed.\n        Returns True if Tesseract is installed or was installed successfully, False otherwise.\n        """\n        tesseract_path = shutil.which(DependencyManager.TESSERACT_CMD)\n        if tesseract_path:\n            print(f"[OK] Tesseract found at {tesseract_path}.")\n            return True\n\n        print("[INFO] Tesseract not found. Installing Tesseract via Homebrew...")\n        brew_path = shutil.which("brew")\n        if not brew_path:\n            print("[ERROR] Homebrew is not installed. Cannot install Tesseract.")\n            return False\n\n        try:\n            subprocess.run([brew_path, "install", "tesseract"], check=True)\n            print("[OK] Tesseract installed successfully.")\n            return True\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n            print("[ERROR] Failed to install Tesseract. Please install it manually: brew install tesseract")\n            return False\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def install_pip_dependencies(requirements_file: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:\n        """\n        Installs pip dependencies from requirements.txt.\n        Returns True if installation succeeded, False otherwise.\n        """\n        req_file = requirements_file or DependencyManager.REQUIREMENTS_FILE\n        print(f"[INFO] Installing pip dependencies from {req_file}...")\n        try:\n            subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "--upgrade", "pip"], check=True)\n            subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "-r", req_file], check=True)\n            print("[OK] Pip dependencies installed successfully.")\n            return True\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n            print(f"[ERROR] Failed to install pip dependencies from {req_file}.")\n            return False\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def verify_installations() -> bool:\n        """\n        Verifies that all dependencies are installed and available.\n        Returns True if all checks pass, False otherwise.\n        """\n        # Check Python version\n        if not DependencyManager.check_python_version():\n            return False\n\n        # Check Homebrew\n        if not DependencyManager.install_homebrew():\n            return False\n\n        # Check Tesseract\n        if not DependencyManager.install_tesseract():\n            return False\n\n        # Check pip dependencies\n        if not DependencyManager.install_pip_dependencies():\n            return False\n\n        # Uses Pillow (PIL) for image handling and Quartz (via PyObjC) for high-DPI screen capture. Provides capture_screen(region=None) and handle_retina_scaling(img) methods."\n        }\n    },\n    {\n        "command_name": "Plan.finish_current_task",\n        "args": {}\n    },\n    {\n        "command_name": "RoleZero.reply_to_human",\n        "args": {\n            "content": "Task 2 completed: screenshot_util.py has been implemented to capture screenshots with Retina scaling support on macOS. Proceeding to implement ocr_engine.py for OCR text recognition."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/quiz_automation_mac_1766905667/screenshot_util.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport sys\nimport platform\nfrom typing import Optional, Tuple\nfrom PIL import Image\nimport numpy as np\n\n# PyObjC is required for macOS screen capture\ntry:\n    import Quartz\nexcept ImportError:\n    raise ImportError("PyObjC (Quartz) is required for macOS screenshot functionality. Please install it via pip.")\n\nclass ScreenshotUtil:\n    """\n    Utility class for capturing screenshots on macOS with Retina scaling support.\n    """\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def capture_screen(region: Optional[Tuple[int, int, int, int]] = None) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specified region.\n        Args:\n            region: (left, top, width, height) or None for full screen.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object of the screenshot.\n        """\n        # Get main display ID\n        display_id = Quartz.CGMainDisplayID()\n        # Get display bounds\n        display_bounds = Quartz.CGDisplayBounds(display_id)\n        width = int(display_bounds.size.width)\n        height = int(display_bounds.size.height)\n\n        # If region is specified, use it; otherwise, capture full screen\n        if region:\n            left, top, w, h = region\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(left, top, w, h)\n        else:\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(0, 0, width, height)\n\n        # Capture the image\n        image_ref = Quartz.CGWindowListCreateImage(\n            capture_rect,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly,\n            Quartz.kCGNullWindowID,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowImageDefault\n        )\n\n        if not image_ref:\n            raise RuntimeError("Failed to capture screen image.")\n\n        # Convert CGImageRef to numpy array\n        width = Quartz.CGImageGetWidth(image_ref)\n        height = Quartz.CGImageGetHeight(image_ref)\n        bytes_per_row = Quartz.CGImageGetBytesPerRow(image_ref)\n        data_provider = Quartz.CGImageGetDataProvider(image_ref)\n        data = Quartz.CGDataProviderCopyData(data_provider)\n        buffer = bytes(data)\n        # RGBA format\n        img_array = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.uint8)\n        img_array = img_array.reshape((height, bytes_per_row // 4, 4))\n        # Remove padding if any\n        img_array = img_array[:, :width, :]\n\n        # Convert to PIL Image\n        pil_img = Image.fromarray(img_array, \'RGBA\')\n        return pil_img\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_retina_scaling(img: Image.Image) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Handles Retina scaling for screenshots.\n        Args:\n            img: PIL.Image object.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object, scaled to standard DPI if needed.\n        """\n        # On Retina displays, images may be 2x or 3x the logical screen size.\n        # We check the DPI and scale down if necessary.\n        # PIL does not always set DPI, so we use image size heuristics.\n\n        # For most macOS Retina screens, scale factor is 2.\n        # If the image mode is RGBA and the size is larger than expected, scale down.\n        # This function assumes the user will pass the full screen image.\n\n        # No direct DPI info, so we just return the image as-is.\n        # If the user wants to scale, they can do so by passing a region or resizing.\n        # For demonstration, let\'s provide a scale-down to 0.5x if the image is likely Retina.\n\n        # Heuristic: If width > 3000, assume Retina and scale down.\n        if img.width > 3000:\n            scaled_img = img.resize((img.width // 2, img.height // 2), Image.LANCZOS)\n            return scaled_img\n        return img\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Optional\nfrom pynput import keyboard\n\n\nclass HotkeyListener:\n    """\n    Listens for a customizable hotkey and triggers the provided callback.\n    Uses pynput for cross-platform hotkey listening (macOS compatible).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, hotkey: str, callback: Callable):\n        """\n        Initializes the hotkey listener.\n        Args:\n            hotkey: Hotkey string, e.g., \'cmd+shift+o\'\n            callback: Function to call when hotkey is pressed\n        """\n        self.hotkey = hotkey.lower()\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.listener: Optional[keyboard.Listener] = None\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._hotkey_set = self._parse_hotkey(self.hotkey)\n        self._pressed_keys = set()\n\n    def _parse_hotkey(self, hotkey_str: str):\n        """\n        Parses the hotkey string into a set of pynput key objects.\n        Supports \'cmd\', \'ctrl\', \'alt\', \'shift\', and single character keys.\n        """\n        key_map = {\n            \'cmd\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'command\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'ctrl\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'control\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'alt\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'option\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'shift\': keyboard.Key.shift,\n        }\n        keys = set()\n        for part in hotkey_str.split(\'+\'):\n            part = part.strip()\n            if part in key_map:\n                keys.add(key_map[part])\n            elif len(part) == 1:\n                keys.add(keyboard.KeyCode.from_char(part))\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported hotkey part: {part}")\n        return keys\n\n    def _on_press(self, key):\n        self._pressed_keys.add(key)\n        if self._hotkey_set.issubset(self._pressed_keys):\n            # Hotkey pressed\n            self.callback()\n\n    def _on_release(self, key):\n        if key in self._pressed_keys:\n            self._pressed_keys.remove(key)\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Starts listening for the hotkey in a non-blocking thread.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self.listener = keyboard.Listener(\n            on_press=self._on_press,\n            on_release=self._on_release\n        )\n        self.listener.start()\n        print(f"[INFO] Hotkey listener started. Waiting for hotkey: {self.hotkey}")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Stops listening for the hotkey.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self.listener is not None:\n            self.listener.stop()\n            self.listener = None\n        print("[INFO] Hotkey listener stopped.")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Example usage: listen for Cmd+Shift+O and print a message\n    def test_callback():\n        print("[HOTKEY] Hotkey pressed! an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Otherwise, finish current task by Plan.finish_current_task explicitly.\n3. Each time you finish a task, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to report your progress.\n4. Please install it via pip.")\n\nclass ScreenshotUtil:\n    """\n    Utility class for capturing screenshots on macOS with Retina scaling support.\n    """\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def capture_screen(region: Optional[Tuple[int, int, int, int]] = None) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specified region.\n        Args:\n            region: (left, top, width, height) or None for full screen.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object of the screenshot.\n        """\n        # Get main display ID\n        display_id = Quartz.CGMainDisplayID()\n        # Get display bounds\n        display_bounds = Quartz.CGDisplayBounds(display_id)\n        width = int(display_bounds.size.width)\n        height = int(display_bounds.size.height)\n\n        # If region is specified, use it; otherwise, capture full screen\n        if region:\n            left, top, w, h = region\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(left, top, w, h)\n        else:\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(0, 0, width, height)\n\n        # Capture the image\n        image_ref = Quartz.CGWindowListCreateImage(\n            capture_rect,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly,\n            Quartz.kCGNullWindowID,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowImageDefault\n        )\n\n        if not image_ref:\n            raise RuntimeError("Failed to capture screen image.")\n\n        # Convert CGImageRef to numpy array\n        width = Quartz.CGImageGetWidth(image_ref)\n        height = Quartz.CGImageGetHeight(image_ref)\n        bytes_per_row = Quartz.CGImageGetBytesPerRow(image_ref)\n        data_provider = Quartz.CGImageGetDataProvider(image_ref)\n        data = Quartz.CGDataProviderCopyData(data_provider)\n        buffer = bytes(data)\n        # RGBA format\n        img_array = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.uint8)\n        img_array = img_array.reshape((height, bytes_per_row // 4, 4))\n        # Remove padding if any\n        img_array = img_array[:, :width, :]\n\n        # Convert to PIL Image\n        pil_img = Image.fromarray(img_array, \'RGBA\')\n        return pil_img\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_retina_scaling(img: Image.Image) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Handles Retina scaling for screenshots.\n        Args:\n            img: PIL.Image object.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object, scaled to standard DPI if needed.\n        """\n        # On Retina displays, images may be 2x or 3x the logical screen size.\n        # We check the DPI and scale down if necessary.\n        # PIL does not always set DPI, so we use image size heuristics.\n\n        # For most macOS Retina screens, scale factor is 2.\n        # If the image mode is RGBA and the size is larger than expected, scale down.\n        # This function assumes the user will pass the full screen image.\n\n        # No direct DPI info, so we just return the image as-is.\n        # If the user wants to scale, they can do so by passing a region or resizing.\n        # For demonstration, let\'s provide a scale-down to 0.5x if the image is likely Retina.\n\n        # Heuristic: If width > 3000, assume Retina and scale down.\n        if img.width > 3000:\n            scaled_img = img.resize((img.width // 2, img.height // 2), Image.LANCZOS)\n            return scaled_img\n        return img\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/quiz_automation_mac_1766905667/hotkey_listener.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport threading\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\nfrom pynput import keyboard\n\n\nclass HotkeyListener:\n    """\n    Listens for a customizable hotkey and triggers the provided callback.\n    Uses pynput for cross-platform hotkey listening (macOS compatible).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, hotkey: str, callback: Callable):\n        """\n        Initializes the hotkey listener.\n        Args:\n            hotkey: Hotkey string, e.g., \'cmd+shift+o\'\n            callback: Function to call when hotkey is pressed\n        """\n        self.hotkey = hotkey.lower()\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.listener: Optional[keyboard.Listener] = None\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._hotkey_set = self._parse_hotkey(self.hotkey)\n        self._pressed_keys = set()\n\n    def _parse_hotkey(self, hotkey_str: str):\n        """\n        Parses the hotkey string into a set of pynput key objects.\n        Supports \'cmd\', \'ctrl\', \'alt\', \'shift\', and single character keys.\n        """\n        key_map = {\n            \'cmd\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'command\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'ctrl\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'control\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'alt\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'option\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'shift\': keyboard.Key.shift,\n        }\n        keys = set()\n        for part in hotkey_str.split(\'+\'):\n            part = part.strip()\n            if part in key_map:\n                keys.add(key_map[part])\n            elif len(part) == 1:\n                keys.add(keyboard.KeyCode.from_char(part))\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported hotkey part: {part}")\n        return keys\n\n    def _on_press(self, key):\n        self._pressed_keys.add(key)\n        if self._hotkey_set.issubset(self._pressed_keys):\n            # Hotkey pressed\n            self.callback()\n\n    def _on_release(self, key):\n        if key in self._pressed_keys:\n            self._pressed_keys.remove(key)\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Starts listening for the hotkey in a non-blocking thread.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self.listener = keyboard.Listener(\n            on_press=self._on_press,\n            on_release=self._on_release\n        )\n        self.listener.start()\n        print(f"[INFO] Hotkey listener started. Waiting for hotkey: {self.hotkey}")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Stops listening for the hotkey.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self.listener is not None:\n            self.listener.stop()\n            self.listener = None\n        print("[INFO] Hotkey listener stopped.")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Example usage: listen for Cmd+Shift+O and print a message\n    def test_callback():\n        print("[HOTKEY] Hotkey pressed! Triggering automation...")\n\n    hotkey = "cmd+shift+o"\n    listener = HotkeyListener(hotkey, test_callback)\n    listener.start_listening()\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            pass  # Keep main thread alive\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        listener.stop_listening()\n        print("Exiting hotkey listener.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Using default config.")\n            return Config.DEFAULT_CONFIG.copy()\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def save_config(config: Dict[str, Any], path: str) -> None:\n        """\n        Saves configuration to a JSON file.\n        Args:\n            config: Config dictionary.\n            path: Path to the config file.\n        """\n        try:\n            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                json.dump(config, f, indent=4)\n            print(f"[OK] Config saved to {path}.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[ERROR] Failed to save config to {path}: {e}")\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_hotkey(config: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n        """\n        Retrieves the hotkey setting from the config.\n        Args:\n            config: Config dictionary.\n        Returns:\n            Hotkey string.\n        """\n        return config.get("hotkey", Config.DEFAULT_CONFIG["hotkey"])\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_ocr_lang(config: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n        """\n        Retrieves the OCR language setting from the config.\n        Args:\n            config: Config dictionary.\n        Returns:\n            OCR language string.\n        """\n        return config.get("ocr_lang", Config.DEFAULT_CONFIG["ocr_lang"])\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_region(config: Dict[str, Any]):\n        """\n        Retrieves the screenshot region from the config.\n        Args:\n            config: Config dictionary.\n        Returns:\n            Region tuple or None.\n        """\n        return config.get("region", Config.DEFAULT_CONFIG["region"])\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Please install it via pip.")\n\nclass ScreenshotUtil:\n    """\n    Utility class for capturing screenshots on macOS with Retina scaling support.\n    """\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def capture_screen(region: Optional[Tuple[int, int, int, int]] = None) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specified region.\n        Args:\n            region: (left, top, width, height) or None for full screen.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object of the screenshot.\n        """\n        # Get main display ID\n        display_id = Quartz.CGMainDisplayID()\n        # Get display bounds\n        display_bounds = Quartz.CGDisplayBounds(display_id)\n        width = int(display_bounds.size.width)\n        height = int(display_bounds.size.height)\n\n        # If region is specified, use it; otherwise, capture full screen\n        if region:\n            left, top, w, h = region\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(left, top, w, h)\n        else:\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(0, 0, width, height)\n\n        # Capture the image\n        image_ref = Quartz.CGWindowListCreateImage(\n            capture_rect,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly,\n            Quartz.kCGNullWindowID,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowImageDefault\n        )\n\n        if not image_ref:\n            raise RuntimeError("Failed to capture screen image.")\n\n        # Convert CGImageRef to numpy array\n        width = Quartz.CGImageGetWidth(image_ref)\n        height = Quartz.CGImageGetHeight(image_ref)\n        bytes_per_row = Quartz.CGImageGetBytesPerRow(image_ref)\n        data_provider = Quartz.CGImageGetDataProvider(image_ref)\n        data = Quartz.CGDataProviderCopyData(data_provider)\n        buffer = bytes(data)\n        # RGBA format\n        img_array = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.uint8)\n        img_array = img_array.reshape((height, bytes_per_row // 4, 4))\n        # Remove padding if any\n        img_array = img_array[:, :width, :]\n\n        # Convert to PIL Image\n        pil_img = Image.fromarray(img_array, \'RGBA\')\n        return pil_img\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_retina_scaling(img: Image.Image) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Handles Retina scaling for screenshots.\n        Args:\n            img: PIL.Image object.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object, scaled to standard DPI if needed.\n        """\n        # On Retina displays, images may be 2x or 3x the logical screen size.\n        # We check the DPI and scale down if necessary.\n        # PIL does not always set DPI, so we use image size heuristics.\n\n        # For most macOS Retina screens, scale factor is 2.\n        # If the image mode is RGBA and the size is larger than expected, scale down.\n        # This function assumes the user will pass the full screen image.\n\n        # No direct DPI info, so we just return the image as-is.\n        # If the user wants to scale, they can do so by passing a region or resizing.\n        # For demonstration, let\'s provide a scale-down to 0.5x if the image is likely Retina.\n\n        # Heuristic: If width > 3000, assume Retina and scale down.\n        if img.width > 3000:\n            scaled_img = img.resize((img.width // 2, img.height // 2), Image.LANCZOS)\n            return scaled_img\n        return img\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/quiz_automation_mac_1766905667/hotkey_listener.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport threading\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\nfrom pynput import keyboard\n\n\nclass HotkeyListener:\n    """\n    Listens for a customizable hotkey and triggers the provided callback.\n    Uses pynput for cross-platform hotkey listening (macOS compatible).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, hotkey: str, callback: Callable):\n        """\n        Initializes the hotkey listener.\n        Args:\n            hotkey: Hotkey string, e.g., \'cmd+shift+o\'\n            callback: Function to call when hotkey is pressed\n        """\n        self.hotkey = hotkey.lower()\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.listener: Optional[keyboard.Listener] = None\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._hotkey_set = self._parse_hotkey(self.hotkey)\n        self._pressed_keys = set()\n\n    def _parse_hotkey(self, hotkey_str: str):\n        """\n        Parses the hotkey string into a set of pynput key objects.\n        Supports \'cmd\', \'ctrl\', \'alt\', \'shift\', and single character keys.\n        """\n        key_map = {\n            \'cmd\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'command\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'ctrl\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'control\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'alt\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'option\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'shift\': keyboard.Key.shift,\n        }\n        keys = set()\n        for part in hotkey_str.split(\'+\'):\n            part = part.strip()\n            if part in key_map:\n                keys.add(key_map[part])\n            elif len(part) == 1:\n                keys.add(keyboard.KeyCode.from_char(part))\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported hotkey part: {part}")\n        return keys\n\n    def _on_press(self, key):\n        self._pressed_keys.add(key)\n        if self._hotkey_set.issubset(self._pressed_keys):\n            # Hotkey pressed\n            self.callback()\n\n    def _on_release(self, key):\n        if key in self._pressed_keys:\n            self._pressed_keys.remove(key)\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Starts listening for the hotkey in a non-blocking thread.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self.listener = keyboard.Listener(\n            on_press=self._on_press,\n            on_release=self._on_release\n        )\n        self.listener.start()\n        print(f"[INFO] Hotkey listener started. Waiting for hotkey: {self.hotkey}")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Stops listening for the hotkey.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self.listener is not None:\n            self.listener.stop()\n            self.listener = None\n        print("[INFO] Hotkey listener stopped.")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Example usage: listen for Cmd+Shift+O and print a message\n    def test_callback():\n        print("[HOTKEY] Hotkey pressed! Triggering automation...")\n\n    hotkey = "cmd+shift+o"\n    listener = HotkeyListener(hotkey, test_callback)\n    listener.start_listening()\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            pass  # Keep main thread alive\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        listener.stop_listening()\n        print("Exiting hotkey listener.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/quiz_automation_mac_1766905667/setup.sh has been successfully created, with content:
#!/bin/bash

# setup.sh - Automated setup for quiz_automation_mac on macOS
# Installs Homebrew, Tesseract, Python 3, and all Python dependencies from requirements.txt

set -e

echo "=== Quiz Automation for macOS: Setup Script ==="

# Function to check command existence
command_exists() {
    command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

# 1. Check Python version (3.9+)
PYTHON_BIN=$(which python3 || true)
if [ -z "$PYTHON_BIN" ]; then
    echo "[ERROR] Python3 not found. Please install Python 3.9 or newer from https://www.python.org/downloads/"
    exit 1
fi

PYTHON_VERSION=$($PYTHON_BIN -c 'import sys; print(f"{sys.version_info.major}.{sys.version_info.minor}")')
PYTHON_MAJOR=$(echo $PYTHON_VERSION | cut -d. -f1)
PYTHON_MINOR=$(echo $PYTHON_VERSION | cut -d. -f2)
if [ "$PYTHON_MAJOR" -lt 3 ] || [ "$PYTHON_MINOR" -lt 9 ]; then
    echo "[ERROR] Python 3.9+ is required. Found Python $PYTHON_VERSION."
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] Python $PYTHON_VERSION detected."

# 2. Install Homebrew if missing
if ! command_exists brew; then
    echo "[INFO] Homebrew not found. Installing Homebrew..."
    /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
    echo "[OK] Homebrew installed."
else
    echo "[OK] Homebrew is already installed."
fi

# 3. Install Tesseract if missing
if ! command_exists tesseract; then
    echo "[INFO] Tesseract not found. Installing Tesseract via Homebrew..."
    brew install tesseract
    echo "[OK] Tesseract installed."
else
    echo "[OK] Tesseract is already installed."
fi

# 4. Upgrade pip
echo "[INFO] Upgrading pip..."
$PYTHON_BIN -m pip install --upgrade pip

# 5. Install Python dependencies
REQ_FILE="requirements.txt"
if [ ! -f "$REQ_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[ERROR] requirements.txt not found in current directory."
    exit 1
fi

echo "[INFO] Installing Python dependencies from $REQ_FILE..."
$PYTHON_BIN -m pip install -r "$REQ_FILE"
echo "[OK] Python dependencies installed."

# 6. Verify installation
echo "[INFO] Verifying installation..."
$PYTHON_BIN -c "import pytesseract, PIL, pynput, numpy; print('[OK] All Python dependencies are installed.')"

echo "=== Setup Complete! ==="
echo "You can now run: python3 main.py"

Installing Homebrew...")\n        try:\n            # Official Homebrew install command\n            subprocess.run(\n                \'/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"\',\n                shell=True, check=True)\n            print("[OK] Homebrew installed successfully.")\n            return True\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n            print("[ERROR] Failed to install Homebrew. Please install it manually from https://brew.sh/")\n            return False\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def install_tesseract() -> bool:\n        """\n        Installs Tesseract OCR using Homebrew if not already installed.\n        Returns True if Tesseract is installed or was installed successfully, False otherwise.\n        """\n        tesseract_path = shutil.which(DependencyManager.TESSERACT_CMD)\n        if tesseract_path:\n            print(f"[OK] Tesseract found at {tesseract_path}.")\n            return True\n\n        print("[INFO] Tesseract not found. Installing Tesseract via Homebrew...")\n        brew_path = shutil.which("brew")\n        if not brew_path:\n            print("[ERROR] Homebrew is not installed. Cannot install Tesseract.")\n            return False\n\n        try:\n            subprocess.run([brew_path, "install", "tesseract"], check=True)\n            print("[OK] Tesseract installed successfully.")\n            return True\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n            print("[ERROR] Failed to install Tesseract. Please install it via pip.")\n\nclass ScreenshotUtil:\n    """\n    Utility class for capturing screenshots on macOS with Retina scaling support.\n    """\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def capture_screen(region: Optional[Tuple[int, int, int, int]] = None) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specified region.\n        Args:\n            region: (left, top, width, height) or None for full screen.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object of the screenshot.\n        """\n        # Get main display ID\n        display_id = Quartz.CGMainDisplayID()\n        # Get display bounds\n        display_bounds = Quartz.CGDisplayBounds(display_id)\n        width = int(display_bounds.size.width)\n        height = int(display_bounds.size.height)\n\n        # If region is specified, use it; otherwise, capture full screen\n        if region:\n            left, top, w, h = region\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(left, top, w, h)\n        else:\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(0, 0, width, height)\n\n        # Capture the image\n        image_ref = Quartz.CGWindowListCreateImage(\n            capture_rect,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly,\n            Quartz.kCGNullWindowID,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowImageDefault\n        )\n\n        if not image_ref:\n            raise RuntimeError("Failed to capture screen image.")\n\n        # Convert CGImageRef to numpy array\n        width = Quartz.CGImageGetWidth(image_ref)\n        height = Quartz.CGImageGetHeight(image_ref)\n        bytes_per_row = Quartz.CGImageGetBytesPerRow(image_ref)\n        data_provider = Quartz.CGImageGetDataProvider(image_ref)\n        data = Quartz.CGDataProviderCopyData(data_provider)\n        buffer = bytes(data)\n        # RGBA format\n        img_array = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.uint8)\n        img_array = img_array.reshape((height, bytes_per_row // 4, 4))\n        # Remove padding if any\n        img_array = img_array[:, :width, :]\n\n        # Convert to PIL Image\n        pil_img = Image.fromarray(img_array, \'RGBA\')\n        return pil_img\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_retina_scaling(img: Image.Image) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Handles Retina scaling for screenshots.\n        Args:\n            img: PIL.Image object.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object, scaled to standard DPI if needed.\n        """\n        # On Retina displays, images may be 2x or 3x the logical screen size.\n        # We check the DPI and scale down if necessary.\n        # PIL does not always set DPI, so we use image size heuristics.\n\n        # For most macOS Retina screens, scale factor is 2.\n        # If the image mode is RGBA and the size is larger than expected, scale down.\n        # This function assumes the user will pass the full screen image.\n\n        # No direct DPI info, so we just return the image as-is.\n        # If the user wants to scale, they can do so by passing a region or resizing.\n        # For demonstration, let\'s provide a scale-down to 0.5x if the image is likely Retina.\n\n        # Heuristic: If width > 3000, assume Retina and scale down.\n        if img.width > 3000:\n            scaled_img = img.resize((img.width // 2, img.height // 2), Image.LANCZOS)\n            return scaled_img\n        return img\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Optional\nfrom pynput import keyboard\n\n\nclass HotkeyListener:\n    """\n    Listens for a customizable hotkey and triggers the provided callback.\n    Uses pynput for cross-platform hotkey listening (macOS compatible).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, hotkey: str, callback: Callable):\n        """\n        Initializes the hotkey listener.\n        Args:\n            hotkey: Hotkey string, e.g., \'cmd+shift+o\'\n            callback: Function to call when hotkey is pressed\n        """\n        self.hotkey = hotkey.lower()\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.listener: Optional[keyboard.Listener] = None\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._hotkey_set = self._parse_hotkey(self.hotkey)\n        self._pressed_keys = set()\n\n    def _parse_hotkey(self, hotkey_str: str):\n        """\n        Parses the hotkey string into a set of pynput key objects.\n        Supports \'cmd\', \'ctrl\', \'alt\', \'shift\', and single character keys.\n        """\n        key_map = {\n            \'cmd\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'command\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'ctrl\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'control\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'alt\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'option\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'shift\': keyboard.Key.shift,\n        }\n        keys = set()\n        for part in hotkey_str.split(\'+\'):\n            part = part.strip()\n            if part in key_map:\n                keys.add(key_map[part])\n            elif len(part) == 1:\n                keys.add(keyboard.KeyCode.from_char(part))\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported hotkey part: {part}")\n        return keys\n\n    def _on_press(self, key):\n        self._pressed_keys.add(key)\n        if self._hotkey_set.issubset(self._pressed_keys):\n            # Hotkey pressed\n            self.callback()\n\n    def _on_release(self, key):\n        if key in self._pressed_keys:\n            self._pressed_keys.remove(key)\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Starts listening for the hotkey in a non-blocking thread.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self.listener = keyboard.Listener(\n            on_press=self._on_press,\n            on_release=self._on_release\n        )\n        self.listener.start()\n        print(f"[INFO] Hotkey listener started. Waiting for hotkey: {self.hotkey}")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Stops listening for the hotkey.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self.listener is not None:\n            self.listener.stop()\n            self.listener = None\n        print("[INFO] Hotkey listener stopped.")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Example usage: listen for Cmd+Shift+O and print a message\n    def test_callback():\n        print("[HOTKEY] Hotkey pressed! Triggering automation...")\n\n    hotkey = "cmd+shift+o"\n    listener = HotkeyListener(hotkey, test_callback)\n    listener.start_listening()\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            pass  # Keep main thread alive\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        listener.stop_listening()\n        print("Exiting hotkey listener.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Please install it via pip.")\n\nclass ScreenshotUtil:\n    """\n    Utility class for capturing screenshots on macOS with Retina scaling support.\n    """\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def capture_screen(region: Optional[Tuple[int, int, int, int]] = None) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specified region.\n        Args:\n            region: (left, top, width, height) or None for full screen.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object of the screenshot.\n        """\n        # Get main display ID\n        display_id = Quartz.CGMainDisplayID()\n        # Get display bounds\n        display_bounds = Quartz.CGDisplayBounds(display_id)\n        width = int(display_bounds.size.width)\n        height = int(display_bounds.size.height)\n\n        # If region is specified, use it; otherwise, capture full screen\n        if region:\n            left, top, w, h = region\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(left, top, w, h)\n        else:\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(0, 0, width, height)\n\n        # Capture the image\n        image_ref = Quartz.CGWindowListCreateImage(\n            capture_rect,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly,\n            Quartz.kCGNullWindowID,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowImageDefault\n        )\n\n        if not image_ref:\n            raise RuntimeError("Failed to capture screen image.")\n\n        # Convert CGImageRef to numpy array\n        width = Quartz.CGImageGetWidth(image_ref)\n        height = Quartz.CGImageGetHeight(image_ref)\n        bytes_per_row = Quartz.CGImageGetBytesPerRow(image_ref)\n        data_provider = Quartz.CGImageGetDataProvider(image_ref)\n        data = Quartz.CGDataProviderCopyData(data_provider)\n        buffer = bytes(data)\n        # RGBA format\n        img_array = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.uint8)\n        img_array = img_array.reshape((height, bytes_per_row // 4, 4))\n        # Remove padding if any\n        img_array = img_array[:, :width, :]\n\n        # Convert to PIL Image\n        pil_img = Image.fromarray(img_array, \'RGBA\')\n        return pil_img\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_retina_scaling(img: Image.Image) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Handles Retina scaling for screenshots.\n        Args:\n            img: PIL.Image object.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object, scaled to standard DPI if needed.\n        """\n        # On Retina displays, images may be 2x or 3x the logical screen size.\n        # We check the DPI and scale down if necessary.\n        # PIL does not always set DPI, so we use image size heuristics.\n\n        # For most macOS Retina screens, scale factor is 2.\n        # If the image mode is RGBA and the size is larger than expected, scale down.\n        # This function assumes the user will pass the full screen image.\n\n        # No direct DPI info, so we just return the image as-is.\n        # If the user wants to scale, they can do so by passing a region or resizing.\n        # For demonstration, let\'s provide a scale-down to 0.5x if the image is likely Retina.\n\n        # Heuristic: If width > 3000, assume Retina and scale down.\n        if img.width > 3000:\n            scaled_img = img.resize((img.width // 2, img.height // 2), Image.LANCZOS)\n            return scaled_img\n        return img\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/quiz_automation_mac_1766905667/hotkey_listener.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport threading\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\nfrom pynput import keyboard\n\n\nclass HotkeyListener:\n    """\n    Listens for a customizable hotkey and triggers the provided callback.\n    Uses pynput for cross-platform hotkey listening (macOS compatible).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, hotkey: str, callback: Callable):\n        """\n        Initializes the hotkey listener.\n        Args:\n            hotkey: Hotkey string, e.g., \'cmd+shift+o\'\n            callback: Function to call when hotkey is pressed\n        """\n        self.hotkey = hotkey.lower()\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.listener: Optional[keyboard.Listener] = None\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._hotkey_set = self._parse_hotkey(self.hotkey)\n        self._pressed_keys = set()\n\n    def _parse_hotkey(self, hotkey_str: str):\n        """\n        Parses the hotkey string into a set of pynput key objects.\n        Supports \'cmd\', \'ctrl\', \'alt\', \'shift\', and single character keys.\n        """\n        key_map = {\n            \'cmd\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'command\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'ctrl\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'control\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'alt\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'option\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'shift\': keyboard.Key.shift,\n        }\n        keys = set()\n        for part in hotkey_str.split(\'+\'):\n            part = part.strip()\n            if part in key_map:\n                keys.add(key_map[part])\n            elif len(part) == 1:\n                keys.add(keyboard.KeyCode.from_char(part))\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported hotkey part: {part}")\n        return keys\n\n    def _on_press(self, key):\n        self._pressed_keys.add(key)\n        if self._hotkey_set.issubset(self._pressed_keys):\n            # Hotkey pressed\n            self.callback()\n\n    def _on_release(self, key):\n        if key in self._pressed_keys:\n            self._pressed_keys.remove(key)\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Starts listening for the hotkey in a non-blocking thread.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self.listener = keyboard.Listener(\n            on_press=self._on_press,\n            on_release=self._on_release\n        )\n        self.listener.start()\n        print(f"[INFO] Hotkey listener started. Waiting for hotkey: {self.hotkey}")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Stops listening for the hotkey.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self.listener is not None:\n            self.listener.stop()\n            self.listener = None\n        print("[INFO] Hotkey listener stopped.")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Example usage: listen for Cmd+Shift+O and print a message\n    def test_callback():\n        print("[HOTKEY] Hotkey pressed! Triggering automation...")\n\n    hotkey = "cmd+shift+o"\n    listener = HotkeyListener(hotkey, test_callback)\n    listener.start_listening()\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            pass  # Keep main thread alive\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        listener.stop_listening()\n        print("Exiting hotkey listener.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. # Quiz Automation for macOS – Setup & Usage Guide

Welcome to **quiz_automation_mac**! This guide will help you install, configure, and use the Python-based quiz automation script for macOS. The tool captures your screen, recognizes quiz text using OCR, and is triggered by a hotkey. It supports Retina displays and is easy to set up.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
2. [Installation](#installation)
3. [Configuration](#configuration)
4. [Running the Script](#running-the-script)
5. [Hotkey Usage](#hotkey-usage)
6. [Retina Display & Region Selection](#retina-display--region-selection)
7. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
8. [Credits](#credits)

---

## Prerequisites

- **macOS** (Intel or Apple Silicon)
- **Python 3.9+** ([Download Python](https://www.python.org/downloads/))
- **Homebrew** (will be installed automatically if missing)
- **Tesseract OCR** (will be installed automatically if missing)
- **Internet connection** (for dependency installation)

---

## Installation

1. **Download or Clone the Repository**

   ```
   git clone <your-repo-url>
   cd quiz_automation_mac_1766905667
   ```

2. **Run the Setup Script**

   The setup script will:
   - Check for Python 3.9+
   - Install Homebrew (if missing)
   - Install Tesseract OCR (if missing)
   - Upgrade pip
   - Install all required Python packages

   ```
   chmod +x setup.sh
   ./setup.sh
   ```

   If you encounter permission issues, try:
   ```
   sudo ./setup.sh
   ```

---

## Configuration

Configuration is managed via `config.json` in the project root. The script will create this file with defaults if it does not exist.

**Default config:**

}\\n029|    class ScreenshotUtil {\\n030|        +capture_screen(region: tuple=None) -> Image\\n031|        +handle_retina_scaling(img: Image) -> Image\\n032|    }\\n033|    class OCREngine {\\n034| Please install it via pip.")\n\nclass ScreenshotUtil:\n    """\n    Utility class for capturing screenshots on macOS with Retina scaling support.\n    """\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def capture_screen(region: Optional[Tuple[int, int, int, int]] = None) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specified region.\n        Args:\n            region: (left, top, width, height) or None for full screen.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object of the screenshot.\n        """\n        # Get main display ID\n        display_id = Quartz.CGMainDisplayID()\n        # Get display bounds\n        display_bounds = Quartz.CGDisplayBounds(display_id)\n        width = int(display_bounds.size.width)\n        height = int(display_bounds.size.height)\n\n        # If region is specified, use it; otherwise, capture full screen\n        if region:\n            left, top, w, h = region\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(left, top, w, h)\n        else:\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(0, 0, width, height)\n\n        # Capture the image\n        image_ref = Quartz.CGWindowListCreateImage(\n            capture_rect,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly,\n            Quartz.kCGNullWindowID,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowImageDefault\n        )\n\n        if not image_ref:\n            raise RuntimeError("Failed to capture screen image.")\n\n        # Convert CGImageRef to numpy array\n        width = Quartz.CGImageGetWidth(image_ref)\n        height = Quartz.CGImageGetHeight(image_ref)\n        bytes_per_row = Quartz.CGImageGetBytesPerRow(image_ref)\n        data_provider = Quartz.CGImageGetDataProvider(image_ref)\n        data = Quartz.CGDataProviderCopyData(data_provider)\n        buffer = bytes(data)\n        # RGBA format\n        img_array = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.uint8)\n        img_array = img_array.reshape((height, bytes_per_row // 4, 4))\n        # Remove padding if any\n        img_array = img_array[:, :width, :]\n\n        # Convert to PIL Image\n        pil_img = Image.fromarray(img_array, \'RGBA\')\n        return pil_img\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_retina_scaling(img: Image.Image) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Handles Retina scaling for screenshots.\n        Args:\n            img: PIL.Image object.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object, scaled to standard DPI if needed.\n        """\n        # On Retina displays, images may be 2x or 3x the logical screen size.\n        # We check the DPI and scale down if necessary.\n        # PIL does not always set DPI, so we use image size heuristics.\n\n        # For most macOS Retina screens, scale factor is 2.\n        # If the image mode is RGBA and the size is larger than expected, scale down.\n        # This function assumes the user will pass the full screen image.\n\n        # No direct DPI info, so we just return the image as-is.\n        # If the user wants to scale, they can do so by passing a region or resizing.\n        # For demonstration, let\'s provide a scale-down to 0.5x if the image is likely Retina.\n\n        # Heuristic: If width > 3000, assume Retina and scale down.\n        if img.width > 3000:\n            scaled_img = img.resize((img.width // 2, img.height // 2), Image.LANCZOS)\n            return scaled_img\n        return img\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Optional\nfrom pynput import keyboard\n\n\nclass HotkeyListener:\n    """\n    Listens for a customizable hotkey and triggers the provided callback.\n    Uses pynput for cross-platform hotkey listening (macOS compatible).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, hotkey: str, callback: Callable):\n        """\n        Initializes the hotkey listener.\n        Args:\n            hotkey: Hotkey string, e.g., \'cmd+shift+o\'\n            callback: Function to call when hotkey is pressed\n        """\n        self.hotkey = hotkey.lower()\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.listener: Optional[keyboard.Listener] = None\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._hotkey_set = self._parse_hotkey(self.hotkey)\n        self._pressed_keys = set()\n\n    def _parse_hotkey(self, hotkey_str: str):\n        """\n        Parses the hotkey string into a set of pynput key objects.\n        Supports \'cmd\', \'ctrl\', \'alt\', \'shift\', and single character keys.\n        """\n        key_map = {\n            \'cmd\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'command\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'ctrl\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'control\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'alt\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'option\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'shift\': keyboard.Key.shift,\n        }\n        keys = set()\n        for part in hotkey_str.split(\'+\'):\n            part = part.strip()\n            if part in key_map:\n                keys.add(key_map[part])\n            elif len(part) == 1:\n                keys.add(keyboard.KeyCode.from_char(part))\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported hotkey part: {part}")\n        return keys\n\n    def _on_press(self, key):\n        self._pressed_keys.add(key)\n        if self._hotkey_set.issubset(self._pressed_keys):\n            # Hotkey pressed\n            self.callback()\n\n    def _on_release(self, key):\n        if key in self._pressed_keys:\n            self._pressed_keys.remove(key)\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Starts listening for the hotkey in a non-blocking thread.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self.listener = keyboard.Listener(\n            on_press=self._on_press,\n            on_release=self._on_release\n        )\n        self.listener.start()\n        print(f"[INFO] Hotkey listener started. Waiting for hotkey: {self.hotkey}")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Stops listening for the hotkey.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self.listener is not None:\n            self.listener.stop()\n            self.listener = None\n        print("[INFO] Hotkey listener stopped.")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Example usage: listen for Cmd+Shift+O and print a message\n    def test_callback():\n        print("[HOTKEY] Hotkey pressed! Triggering automation...")\n\n    hotkey = "cmd+shift+o"\n    listener = HotkeyListener(hotkey, test_callback)\n    listener.start_listening()\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            pass  # Keep main thread alive\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        listener.stop_listening()\n        print("Exiting hotkey listener.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. The system will use Homebrew and pip for dependency management, pytesseract (with Tesseract-OCR) for OCR, and PyObjC or Pillow for Retina-aware screenshot capture. Hotkey triggers will be handled by the `keyboard` or `pynput` library. Documentation and a setup guide will be provided. All dependencies will be listed in requirements.txt and installation automated via a setup script.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- ocr_engine.py\\n009|- Installing Homebrew...")\n        try:\n            # Official Homebrew install command\n            subprocess.run(\n                \'/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"\',\n                shell=True, check=True)\n            print("[OK] Homebrew installed successfully.")\n            return True\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n            print("[ERROR] Failed to install Homebrew. Cannot install Tesseract.")\n            return False\n\n        try:\n            subprocess.run([brew_path, "install", "tesseract"], check=True)\n            print("[OK] Tesseract installed successfully.")\n            return True\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n            print("[ERROR] Failed to install Tesseract. Please install it via pip.")\n\nclass ScreenshotUtil:\n    """\n    Utility class for capturing screenshots on macOS with Retina scaling support.\n    """\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def capture_screen(region: Optional[Tuple[int, int, int, int]] = None) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specified region.\n        Args:\n            region: (left, top, width, height) or None for full screen.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object of the screenshot.\n        """\n        # Get main display ID\n        display_id = Quartz.CGMainDisplayID()\n        # Get display bounds\n        display_bounds = Quartz.CGDisplayBounds(display_id)\n        width = int(display_bounds.size.width)\n        height = int(display_bounds.size.height)\n\n        # If region is specified, use it; otherwise, capture full screen\n        if region:\n            left, top, w, h = region\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(left, top, w, h)\n        else:\n            capture_rect = Quartz.CGRectMake(0, 0, width, height)\n\n        # Capture the image\n        image_ref = Quartz.CGWindowListCreateImage(\n            capture_rect,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly,\n            Quartz.kCGNullWindowID,\n            Quartz.kCGWindowImageDefault\n        )\n\n        if not image_ref:\n            raise RuntimeError("Failed to capture screen image.")\n\n        # Convert CGImageRef to numpy array\n        width = Quartz.CGImageGetWidth(image_ref)\n        height = Quartz.CGImageGetHeight(image_ref)\n        bytes_per_row = Quartz.CGImageGetBytesPerRow(image_ref)\n        data_provider = Quartz.CGImageGetDataProvider(image_ref)\n        data = Quartz.CGDataProviderCopyData(data_provider)\n        buffer = bytes(data)\n        # RGBA format\n        img_array = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.uint8)\n        img_array = img_array.reshape((height, bytes_per_row // 4, 4))\n        # Remove padding if any\n        img_array = img_array[:, :width, :]\n\n        # Convert to PIL Image\n        pil_img = Image.fromarray(img_array, \'RGBA\')\n        return pil_img\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_retina_scaling(img: Image.Image) -> Image.Image:\n        """\n        Handles Retina scaling for screenshots.\n        Args:\n            img: PIL.Image object.\n        Returns:\n            PIL.Image object, scaled to standard DPI if needed.\n        """\n        # On Retina displays, images may be 2x or 3x the logical screen size.\n        # We check the DPI and scale down if necessary.\n        # PIL does not always set DPI, so we use image size heuristics.\n\n        # For most macOS Retina screens, scale factor is 2.\n        # If the image mode is RGBA and the size is larger than expected, scale down.\n        # This function assumes the user will pass the full screen image.\n\n        # No direct DPI info, so we just return the image as-is.\n        # If the user wants to scale, they can do so by passing a region or resizing.\n        # For demonstration, let\'s provide a scale-down to 0.5x if the image is likely Retina.\n\n        # Heuristic: If width > 3000, assume Retina and scale down.\n        if img.width > 3000:\n            scaled_img = img.resize((img.width // 2, img.height // 2), Image.LANCZOS)\n            return scaled_img\n        return img\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/quiz_automation_mac_1766905667/hotkey_listener.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport threading\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\nfrom pynput import keyboard\n\n\nclass HotkeyListener:\n    """\n    Listens for a customizable hotkey and triggers the provided callback.\n    Uses pynput for cross-platform hotkey listening (macOS compatible).\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, hotkey: str, callback: Callable):\n        """\n        Initializes the hotkey listener.\n        Args:\n            hotkey: Hotkey string, e.g., \'cmd+shift+o\'\n            callback: Function to call when hotkey is pressed\n        """\n        self.hotkey = hotkey.lower()\n        self.callback = callback\n        self.listener: Optional[keyboard.Listener] = None\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._hotkey_set = self._parse_hotkey(self.hotkey)\n        self._pressed_keys = set()\n\n    def _parse_hotkey(self, hotkey_str: str):\n        """\n        Parses the hotkey string into a set of pynput key objects.\n        Supports \'cmd\', \'ctrl\', \'alt\', \'shift\', and single character keys.\n        """\n        key_map = {\n            \'cmd\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'command\': keyboard.Key.cmd,\n            \'ctrl\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'control\': keyboard.Key.ctrl,\n            \'alt\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'option\': keyboard.Key.alt,\n            \'shift\': keyboard.Key.shift,\n        }\n        keys = set()\n        for part in hotkey_str.split(\'+\'):\n            part = part.strip()\n            if part in key_map:\n                keys.add(key_map[part])\n            elif len(part) == 1:\n                keys.add(keyboard.KeyCode.from_char(part))\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported hotkey part: {part}")\n        return keys\n\n    def _on_press(self, key):\n        self._pressed_keys.add(key)\n        if self._hotkey_set.issubset(self._pressed_keys):\n            # Hotkey pressed\n            self.callback()\n\n    def _on_release(self, key):\n        if key in self._pressed_keys:\n            self._pressed_keys.remove(key)\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Starts listening for the hotkey in a non-blocking thread.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self.listener = keyboard.Listener(\n            on_press=self._on_press,\n            on_release=self._on_release\n        )\n        self.listener.start()\n        print(f"[INFO] Hotkey listener started. Waiting for hotkey: {self.hotkey}")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        """\n        Stops listening for the hotkey.\n        """\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self.listener is not None:\n            self.listener.stop()\n            self.listener = None\n        print("[INFO] Hotkey listener stopped.")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    # Example usage: listen for Cmd+Shift+O and print a message\n    def test_callback():\n        print("[HOTKEY] Hotkey pressed! Triggering automation...")\n\n    hotkey = "cmd+shift+o"\n    listener = HotkeyListener(hotkey, test_callback)\n    listener.start_listening()\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            pass  # Keep main thread alive\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        listener.stop_listening()\n        print("Exiting hotkey listener.")\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished.
